description: InterObjectCommunication is used to exchange properties between two SimulationObjects.
Limitation is used to restrict SimulationObjects.
Restriction inherits from Limitation and represents artificially created limitations e.g. speed limits.
Constraint inherits from Limitation and represents naturally existing limitations.
Form is used to describe the proportions of the SimulationObjects.
Position is used to describe the location of the SimulationObjects with latitude and longitude.
SimulationObjects represents a participant of the simulation. A SimulationObject is static and is not able to change its values in the run of the simulation.
SimulationComponent inherits from SimulationObject. A SimulationComponent can consist of several SimulationComponents.

ExternalSimulationObject inherits from SimulationObject. The ExternalSimulationObject is structured analogously to the DynamicSimulationObject but do not link to executable code from another modeling space. It contains a configuration for for connecting external models and systems.

DynamicSimulationObject inherits from SimulationObject. Unlike a SimulationObject it is able to change its values throughout a simulation run. A DynamicSimulationObject can consist of several SimulationComponents.

ExternalSimulationComponent inherits from SimulationObject. The ExternalSimulationComponent is structured analogously to the SimulationComponent but do not link to executable code from another modeling space. It contains a configuration for for connecting external models and systems.
Goal is used to check whether a certain SimulationProperty has reached a value. A weighting can also be stored.
The Configuration element is used to define setting needed for the external components.
SimulationProperty is similar to a classic Property.
SimulationUnit is used to describe the values of a SimulationProperty
CompositeUnit inherits from SimulationUnit. It referencing two or more SingleUnits. An example is the position in the geographic coordinate system with latitude and longitude.
SingleUnit inherits from SimulationUnit. In addition, a SingleUnit can have sub units, e.g. m/h is the sub unit of km/h.
MethodCall inherits from ExternalBehavioralFeature. It realizes the linkage of executable code.
SimulationVisibilityKind is an Enum. It describes the visibility of the SimulationProperties for other SimulationObjects.
SimulationPropertyValueKind is an Enum. It determines whether the values are single, a list or a range of values.
